topic: Hierarchical Knowledge Graphs: Unifying OpenClaw and Obsidian into a Compound Intelligence System
decision: Should AI-native knowledge workers build hierarchical knowledge graphs that bridge personal knowledge management (Obsidian) with agent execution platforms (OpenClaw)?
audience: Technical founders, AI engineers, knowledge management practitioners
narrative_arc: Personal knowledge tools are powerful but isolated. Agent platforms are powerful but amnesic. The gap between what you know and what your agents know is the biggest bottleneck in AI productivity. Hierarchical knowledge graphs bridge this gap — but only if the architecture is right.
original_thesis_target: The compound intelligence advantage comes not from better models but from better memory architecture — specifically, tiered knowledge with trust-weighted retrieval that lets agents access the right knowledge at the right confidence level.
sub_questions: What are the current approaches to bridging personal knowledge bases with AI agent systems?
How do hierarchical/tiered knowledge architectures compare to flat retrieval (standard RAG)?
What is the state of the art in knowledge graph construction from unstructured notes?
How does trust-weighted retrieval (boosting verified knowledge over ephemeral notes) affect output quality?
What are the practical implementation patterns for Obsidian-to-agent pipelines?
